chunks.text: University of California at Berkeley Department of Electrical Engineering & Computer Sciences Instructional & Electronics Support Groups /share/b/pub/hytech.help July 1, 2004 HyTech is an automatic tool for the analys

2004 HyTech is an automatic tool for the analysis of embedded systems. It was developed by Prof Tom Henzinger, Pei-Hsin Ho, and Howard Wong-Toi. For further information: http://www-cad.eecs.berkeley.edu/~tah/HyTech/ EECS Instruction once install

ey.edu/~tah/HyTech/ EECS Instruction once installed HyTech for use by EE291E (Spring 2000). It ran on our DEC Alpha and HP-UX systems, which are all gone now (July 2004). We do not have it on our current Solaris or NT systems. The files were instal

rent Solaris or NT systems. The files were installed in ~ee291e/HyTech and the program was run with ~ee291e/HyTech/bin/hytech. Questions about the Instructional installation may be sent to "inst@eecs.berkeley.edu". EECS Instructional S
@eecs.berkeley.edu". EECS Instructional Support Group 384/386 Cory, 333 Soda inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
